# Transcrição: 01 Lesson #4 Building Social Proof On Ads

- **Fonte:** 01 Lesson #4 Building Social Proof On Ads
- **Duração:** 13:14
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Alright guys, so in this video I'm going to show you the easiest way to build social proof to use that ad in a conversion or a traffic campaign or whichever objective you're wanting to use. So social proof is essentially all of the likes and comments and shares on an ad that would be an indicator to someone in your target audience that other people are interested in your product or your service or whatever it is that you're offering. So they'd see those engagements on their ad and it gives them that social proof that they're looking for subconsciously that other people are also interested in this. So typically before you do your social proofing you would split test your creatives first so that you're building social proof on an ad that you actually know is going to work. So the way that you do this is to split test inside the objective that you're actually going to use so that you can get an idea of which ad works best. So if for example you're wanting to ultimately run a traffic campaign you would split test your creatives first in a traffic campaign so that you could see which ads have the highest click-through rate and the lowest cost per click or if you were wanting to run it in a conversions campaign you would judge it based on which one's getting you the best cost per conversion and best conversion rate instead. So ideally you do that first so that your social proofing an ad that you know is going to get you the results that you want based on the objective that you're using. So once you've done that and you have the best ad creative that you want you'll be able to use that same specific ad, the exact same ad, same post ID and everything across different ad sets. So if you wanted to do it with different targeting or different placements you could even use it in different campaigns and even across different ad accounts and it'll carry all of the social proof along with it. So first we're going to create an ad to use so that I can show you guys what to do. So the important thing to note is that if you're ultimately wanting to run a traffic or conversions campaign for example you don't want to create this ad within an engagement campaign and I'll show you why over here. So we're just going to skip this because I'm not going to use this anyway. So I'm just using a test page for this and then I'm going to make sure that I'm creating an ad not using an existing post for this. Single image ad, let's grab a free stock image, get one of a nice puppy. Cool. We'll pick our image here. So now you'll see over here that this is not like a regular link click ad. I can even add in my text here. Do you love puppies?

And then I can add in a call to action but I don't have a lot of options in terms of that call to action so I can choose to learn more and I can add in a URL. You saw that I've only got a few options in CTA so it's much more limited than a regular traffic campaign. So instead what I want to do is come back out here and change this to a traffic campaign. And I'll just go through here. Once again the settings on the side and the ad set level don't matter because I'm not actually going to run this ad set but if you do intend on using it make sure that you have the proper settings for it. I'm just going to be turning this off as soon as I've created it because I just need it for the post id. So once again we'll pull in a stock image, we'll get our cute puppy again and then puppies.com. Do you love puppies? And now you can see here that it has pulled through the regular headline for that website but I can change this so I can say click here to learn about puppies. And I have a lot more options in terms of what call to action I want to use so I might be sign up for that one. And I can add in a display link, I can even have a news feed link description. ## [06:04 - 09:08]

and then we'll actually just give this a little bit of engagement as well so that we've got it to reference. And we're going to go back to the ads manager and then if you wanted to duplicate your ad set so for example our circumstance where you might want to do this is if you wanted to test different targeting you might want to use the same ad so that you can see which one works better so say you wanted to tag test males versus females you'd want to make sure that you're using the exact same ad so that it's a really fair test. However if I was to duplicate this here so we'll do that into the original campaign click duplicate so here's my ad set we'll just publish that quickly. Let's just select this one campaign. Cool so here's our new ad set that's being copied. Now if I look at the ad in this ad set and I preview it it looks like the exact same ad to the one that we just created and it is the exact same in terms of the ad copy and the image that we've used and the call to action button. However you'll notice on our original ad we had this social proof here and you'll also see if you look in the URL bar that they have completely different post IDs so while they look exactly the same they are essentially two completely different ads. Now with it becomes a problem is if you're running the same ad exactly the same in the way it looks and what it says you're running it twice in two different ad sets you're spending $10 on one ad for example or in this case we're spending $20 so every day we're spending $20 getting likes and comments and shares on one ad and $20 getting likes and comments and shares on essentially a whole other ad when we could be using the same one and spending $40 a day on that same ad and having all of the engagement accruing on the same ad creative. So what we want to do to change that so we've got our original ad here and it's got that social proof on it and we're going to take this post ID from the URL and we're going to come into our duplicated ad set. We're going to pull up the ad that we just created and click edit and then instead of create ad we're going to click to use existing post and then in here we can enter the post ID for the first ad that we created click submit and you'll see there's that like that we put on it sitting over there so that's how we know for sure that this is the exact same ad and we can also check so obviously ideally you don't want to go through liking

## [09:08 - 12:11]

all of your ads so the best way to check is after you've published it we'll just give that a second to go through. Now if we go preview you can see that like is there but in your case we'll go here see post Facebook post with comments. There's our post ID and then this is our original one with the same post ID so you can see that number is exactly the same so we know that we're using the same ad ID and it'll keep accumulating engagement together on the same ad instead of on two different ads. So in the past the way that you would have created these posts to use like that you would have gone into page posts over here and you would click on create post over here and you would create it like this so same thing if we went puppies.com like we wrote do you love puppies. We do have the full range of call to action buttons over here so we can click sign up however what you'll notice is this over here how they've removed some fields from website link post creation so we can't change the heading we can't change the image we can't change the link description and if we click create post you'll see here is the post that we just created and it's pulled through the data from that website which has no image or no proper heading so the only way that I can change this is to actually change the data on the website to change the SEO the meta tags the meta descriptions and all of that meta data on the website. So the best way to do this and to social proof your ads is to create your ads in ads manager like I just showed you before so to get engagement on your posts you can either run them as you normally would in your campaign and it'll accumulate over time you'll get your likes and shares and comments as your ad runs to your audience or you can run it in an engagement campaign like this so we have our ad ID over here and we'll jump back into ads manager now we're going to create an engagement campaign and ideally you'll want to use and you're targeting you'll want to use a warm audience because they'll be more open to your ads and more likely to give you engagement on those ads if they're familiar with your brand but we're just going to leave that blank for now but obviously in your case you'll be choosing an audience that is likely to engage with your ad and then over here you're going to go select post if you don't see the post that you're wanting you're going to click enter post ID and paste that in there and you can see there is our ad that we created ready

## [12:11 - 13:14]

to go and I can click confirm and now when we get engagement on this ad it's going to keep accruing it on this same ad so one really important thing to note guys is that you really don't want to be running your engagement campaign to the same exact audience as your traffic or conversions campaign so if you wanted to run the same ad as a traffic ad to a cold audience you don't want to be running it as an engagement campaign to that same cold audience and the reason for that is that Facebook will typically favor your engagement campaign and it's going to cannibalize your other campaign and it's really going to it's going to make it stall and it's not going to work very well so ideally you want to be using two different audiences one for social proofing and then one to actually run it in the campaign where underlying goal is so that's essentially how you social proof ads and you can use this strategy to get some really good likes and comments and shares on the same ad creative instead of having it split across different campaigns ad sets or ad accounts
